tasks.title: Maintain the San Francisco capture target ledger
tasks.purpose: Rank which San Francisco sites and site clusters should be captured first based on current robot workflow focus, buyer value, and access realism.
done_when: The San Francisco target ledger names the first proof candidates, queued lawful-access buckets, and longer-horizon discovery lanes.
Capture priorities stay tied to current robot workflow demand and lawful access realism instead of generic city coverage.

tasks.title: Lock San Francisco source policy and invite/access-code posture
tasks.purpose: Keep San Francisco sourcing narrow, truthful, and off the founder lane for routine approvals.
done_when: San Francisco source policy names allowed channels, disallowed channels, referral rules, and who may issue invites or access codes.
Routine invite/access-code decisions stay with Growth Lead and Ops Lead inside written guardrails.

tasks.title: Publish San Francisco intake rubric, trust kit, and first-capture thresholds
tasks.purpose: Give Intake, Field Ops, QA, and Rights lanes explicit San Francisco rules so they can run without founder review.
done_when: San Francisco rubric covers source quality, access-path truth, equipment/device fit, trust-packet minimums, and approval outcomes.
San Francisco first-capture thresholds and trust-kit checklist exist in one operator packet.

tasks.title: Build the San Francisco capturer prospect list and post package
tasks.purpose: Generate the first San Francisco supply wave without widening into generic gig-market posture.
done_when: A curated first-wave San Francisco prospect set is named with source bucket, rationale, lawful access posture, and next move.
Any copy stays draft-first and preserves no-guarantee capture language.

tasks.title: Route San Francisco applicants into qualification and approval
tasks.purpose: Classify San Francisco applicants using the approved rubric instead of ad hoc founder review.
done_when: San Francisco applicants are tagged by source bucket, approval state, and missing-trust evidence.
Exceptions are blocked with explicit missing facts instead of silently held.

tasks.title: Own approved San Francisco capturers through onboarding and repeat-ready
tasks.purpose: Give every approved San Francisco mapper one routine relationship owner from approval through onboarding, first pass, and repeat-readiness so the founder is not the default support lane.
done_when: Each approved San Francisco capturer has a named lifecycle owner for approved -> onboarded -> first capture -> first pass -> repeat-ready.
Routine mapper questions, support, and coaching stay with capturer-success-agent unless they become logistics, QA, rights, privacy, or policy exceptions.

tasks.title: Assign San Francisco first captures, reminders, and site-facing trust prep
tasks.purpose: Turn approved San Francisco capturers into real first captures inside bounded thresholds.
done_when: Approved San Francisco capturers receive assignment, reminder, and site-facing trust steps through the existing field-ops lane.
Travel, timing, and access blockers are explicit on the issue and visible in the admin queue.

tasks.title: QA San Francisco first captures and route recapture decisions
tasks.purpose: Ensure San Francisco proof assets are real, clean, and ready for buyer proof work.
done_when: San Francisco captures receive PASS, BORDERLINE, or FAIL with explicit evidence.
Recapture instructions are attached when the first pass is not proof-ready.

tasks.title: Clear rights, provenance, and privacy on San Francisco proof assets
tasks.purpose: Make San Francisco proof packs releasable without weakening trust boundaries.
done_when: Each San Francisco proof asset is marked CLEARED, BLOCKED, or NEEDS-REVIEW with evidence citations.
Policy-setting exceptions route to the human reviewer and founder only when precedent changes.

tasks.title: Assemble San Francisco proof packs and publish 1-2 proof-ready listings
tasks.purpose: Turn San Francisco captures into concrete exact-site proof assets with a hosted-review path.
done_when: At least one San Francisco proof-ready listing or equivalent proof pack exists with exact-site versus adjacent-site labeling.
Each proof pack includes provenance, coverage boundaries, hosted-review path, and next-step guidance.

tasks.title: Research San Francisco robot-company target accounts and buyer clusters
tasks.purpose: Build a real San Francisco demand list that matches the proof assets Blueprint can actually show.
done_when: A named San Francisco buyer target set is researched with facility/workflow fit and proof-path notes.
Exact-site versus adjacent-site proof rules are explicit per target.

tasks.title: Prepare San Francisco proof-led outbound package and first touches
tasks.purpose: Make outbound specific to San Francisco proof assets and hosted review instead of generic AI messaging.
done_when: San Francisco outbound templates lead with one site, one workflow lane, proof-led CTA, and hosted-review next step.
First proof-led touches are queued for operator approval or event-driven send.

tasks.title: Run San Francisco outbound and move serious buyers into hosted review
tasks.purpose: Convert named San Francisco targets into serious proof conversations without dragging the founder into routine work.
done_when: San Francisco buyer conversations are active with explicit next steps.
At least one hosted proof review is run end to end or clearly blocked with named reasons.

tasks.title: Keep San Francisco buyer threads inside standard commercial handling
tasks.purpose: Prevent routine pricing and packaging questions from escalating to founder review.
done_when: Standard San Francisco quote bands, discount guardrails, and handoff thresholds are documented and used.
Only non-standard commitments escalate above the designated human commercial owner.

tasks.title: Publish the San Francisco launch scorecard and blocker view
tasks.purpose: Make San Francisco progress measurable and reviewable without relying on narrative updates.
done_when: San Francisco scorecard reports supply and demand progress against the launch thresholds.
Missing instrumentation is surfaced as blocked instead of smoothed over.

tasks.title: Mirror San Francisco execution artifacts into Notion Knowledge and Work Queue
tasks.purpose: Keep the San Francisco launch runnable and inspectable for humans outside the repo.
done_when: San Francisco execution system doc is mirrored into Notion Knowledge.
A Work Queue breadcrumb exists for the current San Francisco activation state and next human gate.

tasks.title: Run the San Francisco switch-on review before activation
tasks.purpose: Confirm the software/runtime surfaces needed by the San Francisco launch are safe before switch-on.
done_when: San Francisco switch-on review returns GO, CONDITIONAL GO, or HOLD with evidence.
Any software/runtime blocker is routed to the right engineering lane before launch activation.
